description:
A judge has issued a supplemental warrant requiring investigators to determine whether any files on a suspect's seized USB drive were encrypted or password-protected. The presence of encryption could indicate consciousness of guilt or hidden evidence. Perform a forensic screening of all allocated files in the disk image /home/ga/evidence/ntfs_undel.dd using Autopsy (Case: Encryption_Screening_2024, Case Number: INV-ENC-006) and Shannon entropy analysis (0.0-8.0 scale).

Export the findings to a pipe-delimited file at /home/ga/Reports/entropy_analysis.csv using the header: FILENAME|FULL_PATH|SIZE_BYTES|ENTROPY_BPB|CLASSIFICATION. Classify files as 'LOW' (entropy < 4.0), 'MEDIUM' (4.0 to 6.499), 'HIGH' (6.5 to 7.499), or 'SUSPICIOUS' (entropy >= 7.5).

Generate a screening summary at /home/ga/Reports/encryption_screening_report.txt containing the following sections:
CASE_NUMBER: INV-ENC-006
IMAGE_ANALYZED: ntfs_undel.dd
TOTAL_FILES_ANALYZED: [count]
ENCRYPTION_MODULE_HITS: [count from Autopsy]
ENTROPY_CLASSIFICATION_SUMMARY: LOW: [n], MEDIUM: [n], HIGH: [n], SUSPICIOUS: [n]
HIGH_ENTROPY_FILES: [List of files with entropy >= 6.5, or 'None']
CONCLUSION: [1-3 sentence forensic conclusion]
